differentials.name: Tumor treatment with ifosfamide, methotrexate Location:   thoracic vertebra, sacrum, pubic bone, ilium, lower extremity
differentials.raw: Tumor treatment with ifosfamide, methotrexate Location:   thoracic vertebra, sacrum, pubic bone, ilium, lower extremity (calcaneus, tibia, fibula)

context: =  normal physiologic stress applied to bone with abnormal elastic resistance / deficient mineralization Cause: Fracture orientation:   perpendicular to long axis of bone Plain film / CT (1-2 weeks after onset of fracture): √  often normal in early stage of fracture √  cortical linear lucency ← disruption (= fracture line) √  localized cortical thickening √  periosteal new bone formation √  medullary sclerosis (endosteal callus formation) MR: √  zone of low SI on T1WI + variable intensity on T2WI (= discrete fracture line) √  surrounded by diffuse marrow edema (hypointense on T1WI + hyperinten
